word: G1903 - ἐπενδύτης - ependýtēs - ep-en-doo'-tace - Fisher's coat

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἐπενδύτης

- Transliteration: Ependutes

- Phonetic: ep-en-doo'-tace

- Definition:

1. an upper garment

a. John 21:7 seems to denote a kind of linen blouse or frock which fishermen used to wear at their work

- Origin: from G1902

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: From G1902; a wrapper that is outer garment: - fisher´ s coat.
